title: DNS Options Flag is ineffective at replacing existing options
body:
### Issue Description

When running a podman container and specifying --dns-option for an option that already exists in the host's resolv.conf, podman appends the options in such a way as to make them ineffective.  For instance, if you had an existing option for `timeout:1` and set `--dns-option 'timeout:2'`, the resulting configuration would cause the container OS to use `timeout:1` instead of the value passed when creating the container.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Configure the host system resolv.conf to specify a DNS option
2. Create a pod, specifying a different value for the same DNS option
3. Run the command `cat /etc/resolv.conf` inside the container

This is a good working example of steps 2 and 3 together:
`sudo podman run --dns-option 'attempts:3' --dns-option 'timeout:2' busybox cat /etc/resolv.conf`

### Describe the results you received

```
$ sudo podman run --dns-option 'attempts:3' --dns-option 'timeout:2' busybox cat /etc/resolv.conf
search cisco.com
nameserver x.x.x.x
options attempts:3 timeout:2 timeout:1 attempts:1
```

### Describe the results you expected

The DNS options would ideally be replaced.  It would be functional if they were appended to the end instead of the beginning, but that is not intuitive and not preferred.
```
$ sudo podman run --dns-option 'attempts:3' --dns-option 'timeout:2' busybox cat /etc/resolv.conf
search cisco.com
nameserver x.x.x.x
options timeout:2 attempts:3
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Tried getting 5.0.1 working but was unable to get it functional on this system.

comments.body: It should definitely overwrite the settings and no copy the ones from the hosts resolv.conf when explicit options are given.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
